'''Adverbs''' in Aelaki evolved from active intransitive converbs. They are built on a root + '''-te''' frame and must strictly agree with verbal tense.

== Formation ==

Adverbs are formed by attaching the suffix '''-te''' to the verbal root:

: ROOT + ''-te'' → adverb

== Stem Alternations ==

Adverbs have four stem alternations:

{| class="wikitable"
|-
! Alternation !! Pattern !! Example
|-
| [[Positive]] || ROOT-te || ''zadate'' "lengthy"
|-
| [[Comparative]] || Root-C₂-te (reduplication) || ''zadadate'' "lengthier"
|-
| [[Superlative]] || Umlaut vowels || ''zædæte'' "lengthliest"
|-
| [[Negative]] || /F/-infix || ''zafdafte'' "not lengthy"
|}

== Tense Agreement ==

Adverbs must '''strictly agree''' with the tense of the verb they modify. This is accomplished through a set of tense-marking suffixes appended to the ''-te'' frame:

{| class="wikitable"
|-
! Suffix !! Tense/Evidentiality
|-
| ''-tesher'' || Mythic
|-
| ''-tero'' || Past auditory
|-
| ''-teshë'' || Past visual
|-
| ''-teshëm'' || Past inferential
|-
| ''-nkerom'' || Hodiernal auditory
|}

Additional tense agreement suffixes exist for the full TAM paradigm.

== Example Paradigm ==

{| class="wikitable"
|-
! Form !! Gloss
|-
| ''zadate'' || lengthy ([[Positive]])
|-
| ''zadadate'' || lengthier ([[Comparative]])
|-
| ''zædæte'' || lengthliest ([[Superlative]])
|-
| ''zafdafte'' || not lengthy ([[Negative]])
|}

[[Category:parts of speech]]
[[Category:git synced pages]]
